---
title: "SecondSplitData"
author: "Daniel Myung"
date: "2026-04-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, cache= TRUE}
# --- Stacked Data Partitioning ---

# 1. Load the unified stacked data (the 'Council of Experts' predictions)
# Assuming you named your combined file 'depression_stacked_data.rds'
stack_df <- readRDS("depression_stacked_data.rds")

# 2. Set the seed for reproducibility 
# Keeping the same seed used in previous scripts to maintain data integrity
set.seed(12345)

# 3. Partitioning: 70% Training / 30% Testing
# This 70% split gives the meta-model enough examples to see which 
# expert (SVM, KNN, or C5.0) is most reliable.
train_size <- floor(0.70 * nrow(stack_df))

# Create a random vector of indices for the training set
train_indices <- sample(seq_len(nrow(stack_df)), size = train_size)

# 4. Break the data frame into the final two pieces
stack_train <- stack_df[train_indices, ]
stack_test  <- stack_df[-train_indices, ]

# 5. Save the final deliverables for the Meta-Model
saveRDS(stack_train, "depression_stack_train.rds")
saveRDS(stack_test, "depression_stack_test.rds")

# --- VERIFICATION & BALANCING ---

# Verify that the split matches our 70/30 target
cat("Training set rows:", nrow(stack_train), "\n")
cat("Test set rows:", nrow(stack_test), "\n")

# Check the distribution of 'History.of.Mental.Illness' (actual)
# It's crucial that the ~30% 'Yes' rate is consistent in both sets.
cat("\n--- Training Set Class Balance ---\n")
# Assuming 'actual' is the name of your ground-truth column in the stack_df
print(prop.table(table(stack_train$actual)))

cat("\n--- Test Set Class Balance ---\n")
print(prop.table(table(stack_test$actual)))
```
